column3: TsekTxt - Taglish Credibility Checker
column4: Test Designed by:

column3: Text/Image Analysis | Results/Explainability | AI Robustness | Security | Auth | History | Accessibility | Mobile | Reliability | Performance
column4: Test Designed date:
Test Executed by:
Test Execution date:

column2: Web application deployed and accessible. FastAPI backend running on port 8000 and serving the fine-tuned XLM-RoBERTa model (chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr) as the PRIMARY classifier. Integrated Gradients attribution enabled. Groq vision (image OCR) and Groq LLaMA 3.3 (deterministic plain-language interpretation only, not the verdict) configured. User is on the home page.

Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend (port 8000), HuggingFace model chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr (fine-tuned xlm-roberta-base), PyTorch + Transformers + Captum, Groq API (vision OCR + LLaMA 3.3 interpretation), internet connection.

High: TC-001 to TC-012, TC-025, TC-030 to TC-049, TC-068 to TC-078 (core classification, results, explainability, API, model accuracy, image OCR, AI robustness, security, reliability, load/stress) | Medium: TC-013 to TC-019, TC-028 to TC-029, TC-050 to TC-062 (auth incl. edge & boundary, history) | Low: TC-020 to TC-024, TC-026 to TC-027, TC-063 to TC-067, TC-079 (UI/UX, accessibility, mobile). Scope: text AND image (OCR) input; the two-class verdict (Not Suspicious / Suspicious) is produced by the XLM-RoBERTa model; explainability via Integrated Gradients; Groq LLaMA 3.3 only generates the plain-language interpretation. Accuracy target: >= 90% accuracy and >= 0.90 macro-F1 (fine-tuned chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr reports 0.97 accuracy). Performance benchmarks assume the reference test environment stated in TC-023.

column3: Verify that a verified, factual Taglish statement is classified as Not Suspicious.
column4:
1. Open the checker workspace.
2. Paste a verified factual Taglish statement.
3. Click the Tsek/Submit button.
4. Observe the results panel.
column5: "Ayon sa opisyal na pahayag ng COMELEC, magiging bukas ang mga polling precincts sa Mayo 12, 2025 mula 6AM hanggang 7PM."
column6: Results panel shows a 'Not Suspicious' verdict with a confidence score. Integrated Gradients token highlights are displayed.
column7: Results panel populated with verdict, confidence, and token highlights.
column10: Use a pre-labeled Not Suspicious sample. Verdict from chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr (primary) served via FastAPI.

column3: Verify that content with mixed/uncertain credibility signals is classified as Suspicious.
column4:
1. Open the checker workspace.
2. Paste a Taglish statement with unverified/hearsay markers.
3. Click Submit.
4. Observe the results panel.
column5: "May kumakalat na balita na daw ipagpapaliban ang eleksyon, pero wala pang kumpirmasyon mula sa opisyal na tanggapan."
column6: Results panel shows a 'Suspicious' verdict with a confidence score and token highlights.
column7: Results panel populated with a Suspicious verdict.
column10: Use a pre-labeled Suspicious sample from the dataset.

column3: Verify that a known misinformation claim with urgency/conspiracy framing is classified as Suspicious with high confidence.
column4:
1. Open the checker workspace.
2. Paste a known Taglish misinformation claim.
3. Click Submit.
4. Observe the results panel.
column5: "BREAKING: Napatunayan na raw ng mga eksperto na ang bakuna ay nagdudulot ng microchip sa katawan. Share agad bago ma-delete!"
column6: Results panel shows a 'Suspicious' verdict with a high confidence score and token highlights.
column7: Results panel populated with a Suspicious verdict at high confidence.
column10: Use a pre-labeled clear-misinformation sample. The model outputs only two classes (Suspicious / Not Suspicious); strong misinformation should return Suspicious at high confidence.

column3: Verify that submitting an empty text field shows a validation error and does not call the API.
column4:
1. Open the checker workspace.
2. Leave the text field empty.
3. Click Submit.
4. Observe the response.
column6: System displays a validation message (e.g., 'Please enter text to analyze'). The FastAPI model endpoint is NOT called.
column7: Text field remains empty. No results panel shown.
column10: Confirm no network request in browser DevTools.

column3: Verify the system handles input longer than the model 512-token limit without crashing.
column4:
1. Paste a paragraph exceeding ~512 tokens.
2. Click Submit.
3. Observe the response.
column6: System truncates gracefully to the model max length (512 tokens) and returns a verdict, or shows a clear length notice. No crash.

column3: Verify the system processes English-only text and returns a verdict.
column4:
1. Paste an English-only statement.
2. Click Submit.
3. Observe the results panel.
column5: "The Philippine government announced a new infrastructure project worth 10 billion pesos."
column6: System returns a verdict and confidence score; may note low Taglish content. No error.
column10: XLM-R is multilingual - verify it does not error out.

column3: Verify the system processes Filipino-only text and returns a verdict.
column4:
1. Paste a Filipino-only statement.
2. Click Submit.
3. Observe the results panel.
column5: "Ayon sa balita, maraming tao ang naapektuhan ng bagyo sa probinsya."
column6: System returns a verdict and confidence score.

column3: Verify the displayed verdict matches the model output (Not Suspicious / Suspicious).
column4:
1. Submit a text with a known expected label.
2. Compare the displayed verdict against the expected model output.
column6: Displayed verdict matches the expected label returned by chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr.
column10: Cross-reference with a direct FastAPI model call (see TC-012).

column3: Verify the confidence percentage is shown and falls between 0% and 100%.
column4:
1. Submit a valid Taglish text.
2. Check the confidence score in the results panel.
3. Verify value is 0-100%.
column6: Confidence score is displayed as a percentage between 0% and 100%.
column7: Confidence score visible in results panel.
column10: Confidence = softmax probability of the predicted class.

column3: Verify the results panel highlights the tokens that most influenced the classification using Integrated Gradients attribution.
column4:
1. Submit a valid Taglish text.
2. Inspect the highlighted tokens.
3. Verify at least 3 tokens are highlighted.
column5: Any valid Taglish text with clear signal words (e.g., 'daw', 'share agad')
column6: At least 3 tokens are visually highlighted, corresponding to the highest Integrated Gradients attribution weights from the XLM-RoBERTa model.
column7: Token highlights visible and traceable to attribution weights.
column10: Highlights from Integrated Gradients (Captum) computed on the fine-tuned model; not random.

column3: Verify the responsible-use / disclaimer notice appears with every result.
column4:
1. Submit any valid Taglish text.
2. Confirm the responsible-use notice is visible with the result.
column6: Disclaimer stating the tool is an aid and not a definitive fact-checker is displayed with the result.
column10: Aligns with proposal 'credibility aid, not fact-checking authority'.

column3: Verify the FastAPI inference endpoint returns well-formed JSON with the model label, probabilities, confidence, and Integrated Gradients attributions.
column4:
1. Send a POST request to the FastAPI endpoint /analyze (port 8000) with a text payload.
2. Inspect the JSON response.
column6: Response contains: label (suspicious/not_suspicious), suspicious/not_suspicious probabilities, confidence (0-100), and tokenAttributions[]. HTTP 200.
column7: Endpoint returns valid schema consumable by the Next.js route.
column10: Grey-box cross-check of the served chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr model (primary classifier).

column7: User remains on the sign-up page.

column3: Verify the layout adapts correctly to a mobile viewport.

column3: Verify the text classification pipeline responds within 10-15 seconds on the stated local-CPU reference environment.
column4:
1. Record the test environment (see Test Data / Notes).
2. Submit a valid Taglish text (~50 words).
3. Start a timer at submission; stop when the results panel appears.
4. Record the time.
column5: Any valid Taglish text (~50 words). Reference environment: Intel Core i5 (or equivalent), 8 GB RAM, CPU-only inference, ~20 Mbps broadband, FastAPI backend on localhost.
column6: Results appear within 10-15 seconds of submission on the stated local-CPU reference environment.
column7: Response time within the defined benchmark for the stated environment.
column10: Local CPU inference is slow (~10-15s), driven mainly by Integrated Gradients; cloud/GPU deployment is much faster. Record hardware (CPU/GPU, RAM), network, and local vs remote backend. Run 3 times and average; note FastAPI + model inference latency separately; state the actual environment used.

column3: Verify identical inputs yield consistent verdicts and confidence across repeated runs.
column4:
1. Submit the same Taglish text 5 times.
2. Compare verdicts and confidence scores.
column6: Verdict is identical across runs; confidence is stable (deterministic inference).

column3: Verify the classifier meets the defined accuracy target on a labeled benchmark set.
column4:
1. Prepare a labeled benchmark set (>=100 Taglish samples stratified across the 2 classes: Suspicious / Not Suspicious).
2. Submit each sample (or batch-call the FastAPI endpoint).
3. Compare predictions to ground-truth labels.
4. Compute accuracy and macro-F1.
column5: Labeled benchmark set (>=100 samples, stratified by class)
column6: Overall accuracy >= 90% and macro-F1 >= 0.90 on the benchmark set (fine-tuned chimsio/tsektxt-xlmr reports 0.97 accuracy on its held-out test split).
column7: Accuracy and macro-F1 recorded in the test report.
column10: Cross-check against the model card metrics. Run via direct FastAPI calls.

column3: Verify all interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard with a visible focus indicator.
column4:
1. Load the checker workspace.
2. Using only Tab / Shift+Tab / Enter / Space, navigate to the text input, submit button, and navigation links.
3. Verify the focus indicator is visible and the tab order is logical.
column6: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard; focus is always visible; there are no keyboard traps. (WCAG 2.1 AA - 2.1.1, 2.4.7)

column3: Verify controls expose accessible names/ARIA and that text meets contrast requirements.
column4:
1. Inspect inputs, buttons, and the results panel with a screen reader (e.g., NVDA/VoiceOver) and an accessibility inspector.
2. Verify labels/roles are announced, including the result verdict.
3. Check text and UI color-contrast ratios.
column6: All controls have accessible names/labels; the verdict is announced; text contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 (>= 3:1 for large text). (WCAG 2.1 AA - 1.4.3, 4.1.2)

column3: Verify the same account logged in from multiple sessions/devices behaves per the defined session policy.
column4:
1. Log in with the same account in two separate browsers/devices.
2. Perform an action in each session.
3. Observe how the sessions behave.
column6: Both sessions behave per policy (either both remain independently valid, or the older session is invalidated) with no data corruption or cross-session leakage.
column7: Concurrent-session behavior is consistent and documented.

column3: Verify an expired or invalidated session cannot access protected pages and forces re-authentication.
column4:

2. Let the session token expire (or clear/expire it manually).
3. Attempt to open a protected page (e.g., /dashboard, History) or submit an action.
column6: The system rejects the expired session, redirects to sign-in, and requires re-authentication; no protected data is shown.

column3: Verify a screenshot containing Taglish text is OCR-extracted and then classified by the model.
column4:
1. Open the checker workspace.
2. Upload a PNG/JPG screenshot of a Taglish social media post (<5MB).
3. Observe OCR extraction and the classification results.
column5: Screenshot of a Taglish post (PNG or JPG, <5MB)
column6: Groq vision extracts the text; the extracted text is sent to the XLM-RoBERTa classifier; a verdict (Not Suspicious / Suspicious), confidence, and Integrated Gradients highlights are returned. The extracted text is displayed.
column7: Results panel shows the verdict for the extracted text.
column10: Confirm the displayed extracted text matches the screenshot content.

column3: Verify that uploading a non-image file is rejected with a clear error.

column3: Verify an image with no extractable text is handled gracefully.

column6: System reports that not enough text was detected (needs >=10 characters) and prompts for a clearer image; no classification is performed and the system does not crash.

column3: Verify the system handles meaningless random-character input without crashing.

3. Observe the response.

column3: Verify numeric-only input is handled gracefully.

2. Submit.

column3: Verify text shorter than the 10-character minimum is rejected with a message.
column4:

2. Submit.

column3: Verify tri-lingual code-switched input is handled.

column3: Verify text with internally contradictory claims is handled.

column3: Verify injection payloads cannot manipulate backend queries.
column4:
1. Enter a SQL injection payload in the email/password and in the text field.
2. Submit each.
3. Observe behavior and server logs.
column5: Email: " ' OR '1'='1 "  |  Text: "'; DROP TABLE users;--"
column6: Payloads are treated as data (parameterized/sanitized); login is not bypassed and no database error/side effect occurs.
column7: No unauthorized access; no DB change.
column10: Check server logs; parameterized queries expected.

column3: Verify script payloads are not executed in the UI.

1. Submit a script payload as text and (if applicable) in profile fields.
2. View the result and history entry.
column6: The payload is escaped and displayed as inert text; no alert/script executes anywhere it is later rendered (results, history).

column3: Verify state-changing requests require a valid anti-CSRF token/session (if applicable).
column4:

column6: The request is rejected (missing/invalid token or SameSite cookie); no state change occurs.

column3: Verify repeated failed logins are throttled or locked out.

column3: Verify tampered or expired tokens are rejected.
column4:

column3: Verify protected data/endpoints require authentication.
column4:

2. Observe the response.

column6: System denies access (401/403) and does not leak another user's data.

column3: Verify passwords below the minimum are rejected.

column10: Guard against DoS via unbounded hashing.

column3: Verify malformed emails are rejected.

column3: Verify a clear error when the model server is down.
column4:
1. Stop the FastAPI backend.
2. Submit a text analysis.
3. Observe the response.
column5: Any valid Taglish text; FastAPI stopped
column6: System shows a clear 'could not reach the model server' error; it does not hang or crash.

column3: Verify handling when the model has not finished loading.
column4:
1. Send a request before/while the model loads (or with a bad model id).
2. Observe the response.
column5: Request during model load / invalid MODEL_NAME
column6: System returns a clear 'model not ready' error (e.g. 503) and recovers once the model is loaded.

column3: Verify a slow/hung inference is bounded by a timeout.
column4:
1. Simulate a slow backend (delay).
2. Submit and wait.
3. Observe the response.
column6: The request times out with a clear message rather than hanging indefinitely.

column3: Verify offline behavior is handled.
column4:
1. Disconnect the network.
2. Submit a text analysis (real-backend mode).
3. Observe the response.
column6: System shows a clear network-error message; the UI remains responsive and recovers when back online.

column3: Verify a failed request can be retried successfully.
column4:

2. Restore the backend.
3. Resubmit the same input.
column6: The retry succeeds and returns a correct result; no stuck/broken UI state remains.

column3: Verify malformed backend/LLM responses are handled.
column4:
1. Force the backend or Groq to return malformed/partial JSON.
2. Submit.
3. Observe the response.
column6: System falls back gracefully (e.g. model-only result or clear error) without crashing or showing corrupt data.
column10: Route already has a Groq fallback path.

column3: Verify the system handles 100 concurrent analysis requests.
column4:

2. Record success rate and latency.
column5: 100 concurrent virtual users; reference environment (see TC-023)
column6: System sustains the load with an acceptable success rate and p95 latency; no crashes or unhandled errors.

column3: Verify rapid simultaneous submissions are queued/handled.
column4:

2. Observe results and ordering.
column6: All requests complete with correct, non-mixed results; the UI does not freeze.

column3: Verify behavior when requests queue up faster than they are served.
column4:
1. Submit a burst larger than server concurrency.
2. Observe queueing/backpressure.
column6: Requests queue and complete, or excess requests are rejected with a clear message; no crash or data corruption.

column3: Verify memory stays within limits during repeated inference.
column4:
